free_text: Characteristics of an intense neutron source based on
beam from the Oak Ridge Isochronous Cyclotron (ORIC)
Beryllium cylinder 38 mm in diameter and 38 mm long.
A 12.7 mm diameter hole is bored along the axis of the
cylinder to within 6.3 mm of the end, thereby leaving
just enough material to stop the 40MeV deuteron beam

The gamma activities of the punched discs were
Rectangular foils, 50 mm x 120 mm, were
mounted on a support in the shape of a circular arc
76.2 mm in radius, and the whole assembly located
with the center of the arc at the center of the d +
Be interaction region. After exposure for 4 h at a
deuteron beam level of the foils were removed,
allowed to cool for several days, and 6.3 mm diameter
discs were punched from them in such pattern, as to
cover the angular range from 0 deg to 90 deg.
